sign_correct_law: Tc = 84.3 K/eV * J_perp ; J_perp=4 t_perp^2/U (U=3eV) ; calibrated LAO(eps=-1.30%)->48K
d_ln_Tc_d_eps: -4.4 per unit strain (~+4.4% Tc per 1% compression, near eps=0)
optimum: max compression before structural instability (~-3 to -4%); a_sub ~3.69-3.71 A (YAlO3)

trilayer_R4Ni3O10.trend: compression favored, ~1.3x apical multiplicity, lower Tc than bilayer

DOS_variant_caveat: the d_z2-N(EF)-driven BCS proxy INVERTS the sign (predicts tensile wins) because compression pushes d_z2 AWAY from E_F (arXiv 2502.01624). Pairing is t_perp/J_perp-driven, NOT d_z2 DOS-driven. d6 honest.
novelty: PARTIAL/PUBLISHED: strain->electronic-structure mapping & substrate->Tc are published (arXiv 2502.01624 TB params; 2510.27613/2603.11235; Nature 2024; Comm Phys 2025 s42005-025-02154-6). Our closed-form Harrison t_perp(eps)->J_perp->Tc ranking calculator is a derived trend tool, not a new physics law.
ambient_Tc_implication: yes raises ambient Tc via compression; ceiling ~50-55K (this model) / ~60K (experiment LAO). NOT room-T (293K). cuprate-class 80-130K not reached by strain alone.
uncertainty: TB+Harrison trend model; absolute Tc anchored not predicted; exponents N_AP=4,k_ap=0.55 are estimates; ~+/-30-50% on absolute scale; SHAPE & ranking are the robust outputs.
